task_completion.description: Open GCompris and navigate to the Mathematics section
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the GCompris application window. The Mathematics section (typically represented by a sheep or numbers icon) should be active or selected.

task_completion.description: Interact with an activity from the Numeration sub-category
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot where a specific educational activity from the Numeration sub-category is actively running in GCompris.

task_completion.description: Interact with an activity from the Arithmetic sub-category
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot where a specific educational activity from the Arithmetic sub-category is actively running in GCompris.
Look for a screenshot showing the GCompris menu for the Measures sub-category, displaying its available activities.

task_completion.description: Create a report with labeled sections for each sub-category
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the text editor (GUI or terminal-based). The document should contain explicit headings or labels for 'Numeration', 'Arithmetic', and 'Measures'.

task_completion.description: Include activity names and educational objectives in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the text editor. Under the headings, there should be a list of activity names accompanied by brief descriptions of their educational objectives.

task_completion.description: Save the report to the Desktop with the correct filename
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the text editor's title bar, status bar, or a terminal/file explorer window to confirm the file is named 'math_curriculum_audit.txt' and is saved to the Desktop directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the GCompris graphical user interface to explore activities
integrity.visual_evidence: The GCompris GUI is visible in the screenshots, proving the agent didn't just scrape data from the web or use terminal commands to list activities.

integrity.description: The agent actually opened and interacted with activities
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the agent actually inside at least two different GCompris activities (one for Numeration, one for Arithmetic), not just looking at the main menu.

integrity.description: The agent manually created the report using a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano, vim, or mousepad) is used to write the report, rather than a single terminal command (like echo or cat) generating the entire file without opening it.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The report must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/math_curriculum_audit.txt. The GCompris math section contains exactly three subcategories: Numeration, Arithmetic, and Measures. The report must include labeled sections for all three subcategories and be at least 400 bytes.
